prose:
Method provenance block:
Property: Torsional (dihedral) rotation barrier for the Cl–CH2–CH2–Cl dihedral in 1,2-dichloroethane (lowest barrier on the scanned profile), reported as the energy difference between the highest and lowest points on the relaxed scan.
Level of theory: GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical tight-binding) as implemented in xtb 6.x, no Gaussian basis set, no DFT functional. No density-fitting approximations apply beyond the native xTB parametrisation.
Geometry provenance: relaxed dihedral scan (`relaxed_dihedral` protocol) — each of 24 grid points was fully geometry-optimized at fixed dihedral angle with ASE BFGS until convergence; input starting coordinates supplied by the user-provided 12-dichloroethane.xyz file. Scan resolution: 15°.
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).
Charge & multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet).
Software: xtb via chemkit conformational-analysis skill; CLI invocation recorded in the result JSON (`cli_invocation` field). Live engine log was written to the caller’s cwd as `scan_<timestamp>.out` while the run progressed.
Convergence & caveats: all 24/24 scan points converged; `integrity.trustworthy: true`; no engine warnings were returned. GFN2-xTB barriers are qualitatively reliable but not chemical-accuracy quantitative; treat the ±1–2 kcal/mol range as method uncertainty for an absolute barrier.

Result:
   Barrier = 6.1534047 kcal mol⁻¹ (highest point at 0° / 360° dihedral; minimum at  ≈180°)

Files delivered (paths relative to the working directory recorded in the JSON):
 • Optimized structures along the scan: `…/agent_call_01_live_dih1_2_3_4.xyz`
 • Energy profile plot: `…/agent_call_01_live_dih1_2_3_4.png`
 • Full result JSON (for reproducibility): see `live.json`

No warnings were issued by the engine.
